_comment: New York State Teachers' Retirement System (NYSTRS) — Defined Benefit Plan. Consumer-agnostic data only.
systemName: New York State Teachers' Retirement System
administeredBy: NYSTRS — governed by 10-member Board of Trustees
boardComposition: 10 members including elected teacher representatives, appointed investment experts, and the Commissioner of Education

totalMembers.note: ~460K total. Tier distribution: Tiers 1-3 <1%; Tier 4 ~59%; Tier 5 ~4%; Tier 6 ~36% (and growing). Tier 6 now has 100,000+ members.

populations: New York State public school teachers and administrators (preK–12)
Community college and SUNY faculty may elect NYSTRS or Optional Retirement Program
excluded: New York City teachers and administrators (NYC TRS — separate system)
School classified/non-instructional staff (covered by NYSLRS ERS)

socialSecurity.note: NYSTRS members participate in Social Security. NY public school teachers pay into SS. Tier 3 has a SS offset provision reducing pension by half of SS benefit earned during NYS public service — but most Tier 3 members elected into Tier 4, which does not have the offset.
socialSecurity.tier3SSOffset: Tier 3 (original): pension reduced by 50% of SS benefit earned during NYS public service. Tier 4 (which most Tier 3 members elected to join) does NOT have this offset.

tiers.overview: NYSTRS has 6 tiers based on membership date. Tiers 1, 2, 3 are legacy (<1% of members). Tier 4 (joined 1983–2009) is the largest tier (59%). Tier 5 (joined 2010–2012) is 4%. Tier 6 (joined April 1, 2012+) is 36% and growing.

tier_1.note: Legacy tier. Most generous benefits. Can retire at any age with sufficient service. No required contributions.

memberContributions.note: Tier 1 members not required to contribute.

tier_1.normalRetirement: Any age with sufficient service credit; full benefit at any age.

benefitFormula.note: Tier 1 formula: 1.8% per year for pre-1959 NYS service + 2% per year for post-1959 service + 1% per year for eligible out-of-state service (max 10 years, total service not exceeding 35 years).

finalAverageSalary.note: Tier 1 may also use 5-year FAS (if pre-June 17, 1971 membership), including termination pay and non-regular compensation in certain cases.

memberContributions.note: Tier 2 members not required to contribute.

tier_2.normalRetirement: Age 62 with 5+ years of service; or age 55 with 30+ years (no reduction).
tier_2.earlyRetirementReduction: If retiring before 62 with less than 30 years, pension factor reduced based on age at retirement.

over30years.structure: 2% per year for first 30 years + 1.5% per year beyond 30

tier_3.note: Most Tier 3 members elected to join Tier 4 — only those who did not make that election remain in Tier 3. Tier 3 has a Social Security offset: pension reduced by 50% of SS benefit earned during NYS public service.

memberContributions.duration: Until 10 years of credit or membership

tier_3.ssOffset: Pension reduced by half of Social Security benefit earned during NYS public service — does not apply to Tier 4.
tier_3.benefitFormula: Same structure as Tier 4 but with SS offset applied.

tier_4.note: Largest tier. Also covers most former Tier 3 members who elected Tier 4 status. No SS offset.

memberContributions.duration: Until 10 years of credit or membership, whichever occurs first

earlyUnreduced.note: Age 55 with 30+ years — no age reduction

earlyRetirementReduction.note: If retiring before age 62 with less than 30 years of service, pension factor is reduced based on exact age at retirement.

over30years.structure: 2% per year for first 30 years + 1.5% per year beyond 30

finalAverageSalary.earningsCap: No single year's salary may exceed the average of the previous two years by more than 10% (for most members)

memberContributions.note: 3.5% for all years. Unlike Tier 4, contributions do not stop at 10 years.

earlyRetirementReduction.note: Retiring before age 62 with less than 30 years, OR before age 57 with 30+ years: pension factor reduced by age. Tier 5 reduction factors differ from Tier 4.

at20years.structure: 2% per year for all years of service (multiplier increases to 2% at 20 years)
at20years.note: Unlike Tier 4, Tier 5 gains the 2% multiplier applied to ALL service at 20 years — not just years 20–30.

over30years.structure: 2% per year for all years; no 1.5% extension tier (benefit continues at 2%)

tier_6.note: FixTier6 campaign (NYSUT) is actively pushing for reform — specifically reducing early retirement penalties and changing contribution structure. Political pressure significant but no reform enacted as of March 2026.

vesting.vestedDeferredNote: Vested-deferred Tier 6 members (inactive membership — no service credit for 7 years) cannot retire until age 63 UNLESS they reactivate membership by earning at least 1 month of service credit.

brackets.note: Up to NY Governor's salary cap

memberContributions.rateDetermination: Rates recalculated annually. New members: based on projected salary for first 3 years. Thereafter: based on base wages from 2 school years prior.
memberContributions.baseWagesOnlyNote: Effective for school years ending June 30, 2025 and June 30, 2026: only base wages (not supplemental earnings) used to determine contribution rate.
memberContributions.interestNote: Member contributions earn 5% compound interest annually.

normalRetirement.note: Unlike Tier 4 (age 62) and Tier 5 (age 57/30), Tier 6 has no early unreduced retirement option — full benefits ONLY at age 63 or later.

earlyRetirementReduction.note: Any retirement before age 63 results in a permanent reduction — no exception for 30+ years of service. Reduction applies based on exact age and service credit. Significantly steeper than earlier tiers.
earlyRetirementReduction.at55: Substantial reduction; exact percentages in NYSTRS publications.

at20years.note: At exactly 20 years: multiplier increases to 1.75% applied to all 20 years

over20years.structure: 35% of FAS for first 20 years + 2% x FAS x years beyond 20
over20years.note: Once 20 years is exceeded, each additional year adds 2% of FAS

finalAverageSalary.earningsCap: No single year may exceed the previous year by more than 10% for inclusion in FAS calculation (10% cap applies in FAS period)
exclusions: Termination pay (retirement incentives, unused leave payments)
Earnings above the NY Governor's salary
Overtime above the annual overtime limit
Earnings from more than primary employer (for multi-employer members)
finalAverageSalary.nystrsVsNYSLRSNote: NYSTRS Tier 6 FAS remains 5 years — the 2024 law changing FAE to 3 years applied ONLY to NYSLRS (ERS and PFRS), NOT to NYSTRS.

cola.description: 50% of the CPI increase, minimum 1%, maximum 3%, applied to the first $18,000 of annual retirement benefit.

eligibility.option1: Age 62 or older AND retired for 5 or more years
eligibility.option2: Age 55 or older AND retired for 10 or more years

recentCOLA_fy2025_26.note: 2025-26 COLA is 1.2%. For a $18,000+ annual benefit: $18 per month maximum increase.

cola.authority: New York State Retirement and Social Security Law (same statute as NYSLRS COLA)
cola.note: Same COLA law as NYSLRS ($18,000 cap, 50% CPI, 1-3%). Administered separately by NYSTRS.

employerContributions.note: NYSTRS employer contribution rates (paid by school districts) are set by the Board of Trustees annually. Rates vary by tier distribution. 101.5% funded on market value basis as of June 30, 2024 — one of the best-funded public pension systems in the US.

fundingStatus.note: NYSTRS is among the best-funded public pension plans in the nation. 99.1% funded on actuarial basis; 101.5% on market value (June 30, 2024). Investment returns generate ~85% of NYSTRS income. 10-member Board of Trustees.

reciprocity.note: NYSTRS members may transfer service credit to/from NYSLRS and other NY public retirement systems under the proportionate retirement program. Members who leave NY teaching for other NY public employment may transfer membership.

fixTier6Campaign.goal: Reform Tier 6 to reduce early retirement penalties, change contribution structure, and improve benefit adequacy
fixTier6Campaign.specificBill: S.6638 / similar — would increase Tier 6 benefit from 35% FAS to 40% FAS after 20 years of service
fixTier6Campaign.status: Ongoing advocacy. No reform enacted as of March 2026.

researchGaps: Exact age-factor reduction tables for each tier (available in NYSTRS publications/Benefit Comparison Table)
Tier 6 FAS earnings cap specifics for multi-employer teachers
Disability retirement benefit formulas and eligibility thresholds by tier
Community college/SUNY Optional Retirement Program (ORP) details — separate DC alternative
